bugs_found.summary: ProviderChain swallowed provider exceptions
architect _TASK_RE rejected '## Task T01:' headers
architect recovery missed plans/{slice_id}-PLAN.md
builder treated silent-but-successful agents as failures
execute_phase failed slice when resumed task had no SUMMARY.md
verifier ran backticked identifiers/filepaths as commands (Permission denied on stringy/utils.py)
runner silently dropped unhandled TUI commands (skip_task/steer/new_project)
TUI steer (SteerScreen + stuck_option_1..5) posted to queue but runner had no handler; now persisted to STEER.md
TUI skip_task ('k') posted to queue but nothing consumed mid-slice; now drained per-attempt, soft-passes with placeholder SUMMARY.md
stuck_option_1..5 were opaque codes in STEER.md; now translated to actionable hints and option 5 triggers mark_paused + Telegram notify
TUI new_plan ('l') mid-execution was silently dropped; now aborts current plan (FAILED) + re-queues for _idle_loop
pause/stop had no dispatcher event (invisible in TUI); no way to resume once paused (TUI only had 'p' binding)
AgentState.from_file crashed runner on startup when STATE.json was corrupted (ValueError from from_json never caught)
mark_started preserved stale error_message from prior FAILED/PAUSED run — tero2 status showed old error text on fresh run
PersonaRegistry used CWD-relative .sora/prompts/ path — project-local persona overrides invisible when tero2 launched from different directory
Telegram bot /cmd@botname (group-chat syntax) silently rejected as unknown command
DiskLayer.read_plan used str.startswith for path-traversal check — accepted sibling directories sharing a name prefix (/proj vs /proj-evil)
config_writer.write_global_config_section unlinked its flock file in finally — creates dual-lock race where two processes hold flock on different inodes

CoachPlayer read STEER.md on every run but never cleared it — same human directive got re-injected into every subsequent strategic pass forever, and _check_human_steer would have infinite-looped any future phase-boundary trigger wiring
Telegram _download_file 10MB cap bypassed when API response omitted file_size (`if file_size and ...` short-circuits); switched to fail-closed
UsageTracker.record_step incremented _total_tokens/_total_cost outside the _providers_lock — lost-update race across threads; moved scalars inside existing lock

execute_phase never cleared STEER.md after applying it as context_hints — bug 107 auto-generated stuck-recovery text leaked into every subsequent task. Clear after apply, mirror of bug 116 consume-and-clear for Coach

verifier _extract_list used re.IGNORECASE so pytest's lowercase summary line ('N failed in Xs') matched the same pattern as real FAILED test lines, polluting failed_tests with garbage like 'in 0.5s ====='. Garbage then flowed into reflexion prompts as a 'specific test name that failed'. Dropped IGNORECASE — pytest emits uppercase FAILED only on per-test result lines

DiskLayer.read_file caught FileNotFoundError and OSError but not UnicodeDecodeError (subclass of ValueError). Operator-written files (STEER.md, OVERRIDE.md, PROJECT.md) saved in cp1252/latin-1 crashed the runner. Added to except list — degrade to empty string like other unreadable files

UsageTracker.session_summary iterated _providers outside the _providers_lock that bug 118 introduced for writes. TUI reads from the event loop while agent threads insert fresh provider keys via record_step, so 'dictionary changed size during iteration' RuntimeError could surface. Read under the same lock — completes the pair to bug 118

investigations.reason: LLM output doesn't reliably include CRITICAL/NO ISSUES FOUND/COSMETIC markers; harden falls through to 'treat as NO ISSUES' after 2 consecutive — acceptable degradation, not blocking
kilo/xiaomi/mimo-v2-pro:free 'Model not found'; bug 98 surfaces it but fix is provider config

iter-8 logs show bug 101 synthesized-placeholder branch fires for ALL THREE tasks. Agents never produce textual summaries. Not a bug in tero2 — builder prompt issue or agent UX. Placeholder path is load-bearing.

last_green_run: iter-8 @ 2026-04-22T10:23 local — 3/3 tasks passed testbed suite green
phase_B_green_confirmed_by: iter-8 @ 10:23 local (3/3 tasks passed, testbed pytest 11 passed, tero2 net test failures = 0 beyond pre-existing stream_bus/bug8 on main)
iter_8_outcome: GREEN 3/3 — all 6 bug fixes (98-103) worked in cascade: chain logged per-attempt, architect recovered plan, builder synthesized placeholder 3x, verifier ran only real pytest commands. Testbed ended with 3 commits, 11 passing tests.
iter_9_outcome: GREEN 3/3 — reproducibility confirmed after bugs 104-108 wiring. Runtime ~7 minutes, harden_malformed open-observation still fires but degrades cleanly as before.
next_action: iter-9 sanity check, then transition to Phase A (TUI) for remaining budget (~6h)

post_phase_b_bugs.description: Three TDD-first bugs found by code-reading after iter-9 green
post_phase_b_bugs.common_pattern: quiet correctness/security issues that the green headless run never exercised: group-chat telegram syntax, sibling-dir path traversal, lock-file unlink race

bug_66_aligned: 40349bb — bug 102 fix changed skip-with-missing-summary from fail-hard to re-run; bug 66's test updated to assert new contract (spirit preserved: no phantom completed entries).
